reviews.review_msg: Great practical course. Very knowledgable  and caring prof. Pick a right group, as it affects your final grade. I wish I could take more courses with him.

If you have interests, Bus473 is very practical and fun course. Prof brings real-world issues/discussions to the class. He is an easy marker (Avg is B+/A-). Group projects/cases are a bit challenging, but you have weeks to work on them with your groups. So, pick a good team.

He doesn't know how to teach. He teaches you the basics and gives you an extremely hard assignment leaving you to fail. When you don't know how to do something even when he didn't teach it, he makes you feel bad and keeps trying to push you for an answer. Very disrespectful and would not recommend anyone to take this class with him.

Horrendous prof, not worth your time and money!! His lectures are very dry and long, and are often not really related to the assessments. You will have to self-study and complete the textbook questions to pass his course. Compare his notes to other profs, and you will be shocked by how much info is missing on his lectures.

This is an advanced level of required operations management course. The content is great, and very useful and practical. The key is to stay on top of stuff weekly. Prof is great and available to help. Cases are a bit challenging.

He is one of the worse Business profs that I've ever had. His lecture notes have almost 0 useful information for the midterm.  He will go over 1or2 word problems in class, then ask the class to study off the textbook questions that are nowhere remotely close to what he has taught in class to be prepared for the midterm (all long-word problems).

This course was offered as a 6 week online as part of PT-MBA pilot program. It was a very applicable and useful course. The prof is knowledgeable and approachable. Since it is a 4 unit course, it could be better to have it in full semester, rather than intense half semester. All and all, I highly recommend it...

Highly disorganized. His content was confusing with very few opportunitues to ask questions. Lots of wasted class time. Get ready to teach yourself. I would highly recommend a different prof if you are someone who learns from watching examples or through discussion. You will get none of that.
Great smart prof who cared about his class and put questions we worked on in class for exam

Payman is very kind and does his best to accommodate students, for example he made our 30 min group presentation online instead of in-person which was a great help. The course material itself is incredibly outdated and there was too much time wasted during class. The supplementary videos are from the early 2000s and not applicable today.
This course is great, with real life application. Payman is awsome and available for extra help. Just attend the class, and do provided sample exams. You will easily ace this course.

be ready to teach yourself... payman is a nice guy and is funny during lectures but he doesn't explain things clearly. he assumes you already understand everything. half of this semester was done through recorded videos and his examples are from 2005. questions on exams were completely new and not covered in class... gg if you have him

Payman is like a double edged sword. His lectures in general are fun as he can be quite hilarious, but his tests, projects, and presentations are TOUGH. Especially the presentations, make sure you are well prepared and can argue your decisions/assumptions or he WILL tear you apart in front of class. IMO projects are too hard
easy course. great prof. attend the class and participate in discussions. you will easily ace it.

His teaching will always be an inspiration to me. His clarity and excellent use of examples make the material he teaches easier to grasp, even if there are gaps in your background knowledge. Fair marking and clear course requirements. Strongly recommend for all the courses he teaches at Beedie.

I enjoyed this class with Payman. The lectures were fun, and the material was interesting. Do not both to contact him within the last two weeks of the course. He will not respond. Give yourself lots of time for the final project because it is HARD!

In my 5 years at SFU, Payman was easily my least favourite professor. He is very intelligent, but gives no direction and is not accessible outside of class. His lecture slides and teaching method are stuck somewhere in the 90's and could use a 21st century update. If you do take this class with him, go to lecture and pay attention, and good luck

One of the best prof I have ever had. No textbook meaning you HAVE TO attend his classes and review his ppts to do well. Materials are highly simplified and he tends to repeat crucial concepts again and again to make sure everything is clear, and he marks you effort and understanding of materials over the right answers. Aws pro

Payman is no doubt one of the most awesome professors at SFU. His lectures are always entertaining with plenty of real-life examples to help you remember the material. He can be demanding at times, but you will learn a lot from him. I would definitely recommend him.

He knows his material well and tries to be a good teacher, but straight up, he's a d*ck. He'll call you out for answers on material he hasn't covered yet and make fun of you for not knowing the answer. Somehow manages to make students that get straight A's in other OM courses feel stupid. Definitely discouraging, wish I hadn't taken it with him.

Doesn't get better than this. Attend his classes, participate in discussions, if you have questions ask, and make sure you understand the lecture material, then you should be able to ace it. Such a great experience being his student.

I feel this was an unfair class. You really need to know a lot about stat/OM/math before you come in. He just expects you to know it, and tests material that you have not seen and not in textbook at all. It was really a struggle and was discouraging for anyone who works hard but don't have any Operation Management backgrounds.
Awesome prof. Had both BUS440, and BUS473 with him. Just attend the lectures, and pick a motivated group (especially for BUS440), you will be fine. Highly recommended.

Payman doesn't know what he's teaching and so chooses not to help you. 25% of the midterms is completely new content. Finally, he makes prejudice about people's work ethic with no evidence and announces it to the class.

I had Payman for 3 classes in my last year of my BBA and he is by far my favourite SFU Bus Prof.  He loves to laugh and joke around but he is also extremely smart and helpful.  If you actually try in his class he will be there for you 100%.  You'd be lucky to have him as a prof :)
He is awesome. Shares real life example which makes the course more interesting. Select a good team partner as it affects your grade later on .Good luck !!!!
Greatest prof I've ever had in sfu!

Payman is almost too slow in the lectures some times. He makes sure that most everyone can understand the material. He is a pretty easy marker as well and gives reasonable tests. However, he loves to play favorites and can't stand arrogant or aggressive people. SERIOUSLY, be overly nice and really modest or he'll grill you.
Very Nice prof. Case study is extremely helpful and connect to reality. Best Prof I ever Had.

This Prof assumed a level of understanding prior to entry of this class which most students did not have.  His teaching style leaves much to be desired.  Payman did not instill confidence in his students.

Good Prof. but some of his teaching was a little high level for some people in the class. He was available for extra help and questions and he even gave extra help sessions. But this class needs a tutorial.

Payman Jula made simple material very complicated and difficult to understand. He did not provide enough opportunities for students to practice the material. He was never willing to respond to student questions.
Was a great prof; reviewed all the important materials from previous classes. Follow his instructions and you ace this course easily.

Fantastic prof!  Payman is clearly a teacher, not just a professor. As long as you attend class and try, you will learn and do well.  He does play favorites a bit, but all you need to do is participate in class.

I took two of my MS classes with him and I am going to take the last one with him too. It's wonderful that SFU has such a great prof like him. Class participation is a really big deal. Try to understand the materials in class and ask questions if you are not following. Never wait till the last week before the exam.

Extremely helpful. Clear in everything explained. His powerpoints and lectures are organized and fun. You won't be disappointed if you review his lecture notes covered before the next class everyweek and try hard to participate in class.

I thought that Mike Johnson for 336 was excellent...but Payman was even better. It's because of these two profs that one of my concentrations is MS. He's really helpful, and really approachable - though he can be kind of strict towards students during in-class group presentations (but that's a good thing in the long run). Thumbs up.

Excellent prof. Would not have made it through 336 without him. He is extremely concerned that everyone understands, he wants students to succeed, and he makes the material relevant to any major. Outside class he is even more eager to explain things and help students. Thanks Payman!

Great Professor in SFU. Interactive class structure, not boring at all!!! Have to read the case before you go to his class. Exams are fair. A difficult class; however, he tried to make it very interesting, and he succeeded in doing so!
The worst prof I ever took a course with. I think he isbetter off do something else rather than teaching.

if you wanna have a great experience knowing what MS is all about, take it with Payman. he knows his stuff very well. and he CARES about your learning. he's the first prof ever that would actually spend the first 30 mins of class reviewing the material he taught last class. exams and grading are fair. highly recommend you to take this fun class.
Awesome Prof! Really cares about his students and really wants them to learn.  Always willing to help and really dedicated to the course and students! Definatley recommended
Very Knowledgeable prof. extremely helpful, makes 437 an understandable course for the students. Excellent addition to the management science area.
